characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Animated Actors: The defunct official website for theStupid Invadersvideo game has a section devoted to Bud Budiovitch being interviewed about the game's development that depicts the aliens as actors playing characters for the game. Bud the actor is notably a different person from Bud the character, being rather erudite and self-conscious instead of a moronic couch potato. Failure Is the Only Option: With the exception of theStupid Invadersvideo game, their efforts to leave Earth and return to their home planet never succeed. Morphic Resonance: A fair number of the disguises they assume using the SMTV look like human versions of their normal selves, with Stereo's disguise always having two heads like he does in his true form. By the second season, there are even occasions where they retain their true forms' colors when transformed (either on their apparel ortheir skin).

characters.name: Etno Polino
characters.description: Accent Adaptation: In the first season of the French version, he has a British accent. Ambiguously Bi: While Etno is attracted to women, going as far as being attracted to an Indian woman in "Bollywood Aliens" and being upset when she reunites with her lost boyfriend, he is quite touchy with aliens like Candy. He also is not disgusted when Gorgious kisses him on the mouth, only slightly wiping his lips afterwards. Bungling Inventor: Etno's spaceships have a tendency to explode. Klingons Love Shakespeare: While not to the extent of Bud, Etno does have a lot of appreciation and interest in Earth's cultures and its sciences. He is quite fascinated by cockroaches and chess, often incorporates materials from Earth into his inventions, and in "Soap Chick Number One" owned a collection of stamps from Earth. Know-Nothing Know-It-All:Invoked. Etnoisquite intelligent, but most of his alien knowledge is useless on Earth.

characters.name: Candy Hector Caramella
characters.description: Alliterative Name:CandyCaramella Adaptational Gender Identity: In the original show, Candy is an effeminate,Ambiguously Gay, crossdressing cis man. InStupid Invaders, Candy is a trans woman who plans on having bottom surgery once she returns to her home planet. It's answered years later viaWord of Godthat they are non-binary in the show, yet the writers didn't know the language of it at the time. Ambiguously Gay: He shows hints of this from time to time in season one. By season two, however,allsense of ambiguity is thrown out the window. Badass Adorable: Candy rarely has his moments. Butt-Monkey: While all the aliens get their share, Candy probably gets it the worst, especially in season two.

characters.name: Gorgious Klatoo
characters.description: Acid Reflux Nightmare: In "Sweet Tooth Blues", after eating too many sweets, Gorgious has a nightmare where he gambles for his teeth. Ambiguously Bi: While he's shown to have the hots for a female tenant in "First Love", he also seems to have a lot of flirtatious moments with Candy in the show. Belligerent Sexual Tension: Possibly with Candy, as he often belittles and argues with them, but the two have had some questionable moments together (the most obvious one being the part inStupid Invaderswhere he compliments her ass). Big Eater/Extreme Omnivore: Gorgious will eatanythingandeverythingif left to his own devices. Eats Babies: Apparently, Gorgious has no qualms doing this, as he attempts to eat one in "Maybe Baby", earning the disgust of the other aliens in the process, who want to try to study or care for the baby they found. He loses interest when the baby soils himself.

characters.name: Bud Budiovitch
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: Bud is attracted to women in several episodes, becoming smitten with a human women in “First Love” and in “Showdown In Tiny Town”, hooked up with an insect named Ramona. He does however get very close with a male fly in “The Flyling”, going as far as sharing a bed with it while eating pizza and when he is forced to let the fly go he bursts into tears and then gets confronted by the other aliens when the male fly somehow laid eggs all over the house. Going back to “First Love”, he later becomes smitten with one of Candy’s disguises as an attractive woman and then after Candy changes back, Bud massages Candy’s feet. In adventure game even implies that he has aPorn Stashyet the type of porn is never brought up. Covert Pervert: He apparently has aPorn Stashaccording to the adventure game. Dumbass Has a Point: In "Time for a Change", he points out that going back in time to avoid their problems is more trouble than it is worth. After the other aliens ignore him and learn his point the hard way, he remarksI Told You So. Expy: Bud bears a somewhat striking resemblance to Pete Puma fromLooney Tunes, given how they're both tall, orange, dimwitted characters. Even Jeff Bennett's voice for Bud in the English version of season 1 is similar to Pete's. Genius Ditz: Bud is the stupidest of the bunch... except when it comes to stuff he learned fromwatching TV.

characters.name: Stereo Monovici
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: While Stereo’s love life was not given as much focus as the other aliens due to being absent for nearly half the show, the episode “First Love” gives out some implications on Stereo’s preferences after Stereo becomes smitten with a human woman . In that same episode however, Stereo becomes smitten with Candy after Candy uses a woman disguise with the SMTV and Stereo retains that attraction even after Candy admits that he is that woman, with Stereo going as far as to offer to do the dishes for Candy. Badass Bookworm: The two are known to be bookworms. Birthday Episode: His birthday is celebrated in "Dead Funny". Demoted to Extra: The second season reduces him to only appearing in two episodes, both of which only feature him as a plot device brought in at the last minute to solve the aliens' current problem, and omits him from the updated title sequence despite the theme song's mention of "five little monsters" not being altered to accommodate his reduced presence. Finishing Each Other's Sentences: His two heads often do this whenever they aren't too busy arguing with each other.

characters.name: The Professor/Dr. Sakarin
characters.description: Big Bad: He is the main villain of theStupid Invadersvideo game, wanting to capture the aliens for his experiments. Death by Adaptation: "Zero Stuff" ended with him still alive and simply stranded in space, while theStupid Invadersgame ends withhim and Bolok perishing with the rest of the human race in anEarth-Shattering Kaboom. Early-Bird Cameo: Before his proper introduction in "Zero Stuff", he can be briefly seen among the scientists the aliens run into at the research facility in the series premiere "Once Upon a Time". Herr Doktor: He speaks with a German accent and uses some German words. Inconsistent Spelling: His surname is spelled as "Sakarin" in promotional material and the instruction manual for theStupid Invadersvideo game, but the in-game subtitles for said game spell his surname with a silent E at the end.

characters.name: Lucien Bolok
characters.description: An Arm and a Leg: His episode ends with him losing both of his arms. Catchphrase: He frequently states "I'm a professional". Death by Adaptation: His episode ends with him still alive, while theStupid Invadersvideo game ends withhim and Dr. Sakarin perishing with the rest of the human race in anEarth-Shattering Kaboom. The Dragon: He serves as Dr. Sakarin's right-hand man in theStupid Invadersvideo game, attempting to hunt down the aliens throughout the game. Your Mom: One of the Game Over scenarios inStupid Invadershas him make such an insult right before using his freeze ray on Bud.Bolok:Do you know the difference between a wimp and a man like me? Your mama!
